songs.title: ? now the first thing you need

clips.text: Now, the first thing you need to understand is what a drawing actually is. And a drawing, let's use this mountain do as an example, a drawing is not just a little mark like that, but a drawing is actually a set of marks that have some kind of ordering principle to them, a set of marks that have a pattern. And that's a drawing, but any old animal can make a drawing. For example, if you have a little kitty and it's making a trail and you're trying to follow the trail, well then that cat is making a drawing with its paw prints, but it's not necessarily a doodle or a sketch. Now, a doodle is a type of drawing that you make, the type of pattern of marks that you make when you don't have any ideas and are looking for an idea. Oh, I found a cool idea right here. And a sketch is something that you might do to develop that idea a little bit further.

songs.title: ? gekking comes from the band 100geks

clips.text: Gekking comes from the band 100geks, and when 100geks found some graffiti on a wall that said 100geks, they're like, "What the heck does that mean?" And a lot of their music goes, "Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek." And so the graffiti they found on the wall was a little bit like an accident, an accidental find. And the Gekking, "Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek," is a little bit like a glitch, an accidental sound. It happens when you turn an accident back around and say, "Oh, I really appreciate accidents, and I want to create a whole art form out of the aesthetics of accidents." That's basically what Gekking is. And accidents occur in other kinds of art, too. For example, in painting, if you leave a drip and the drip drops down on the canvas, that is also a kind of Gekking.

songs.title: ? sometimes people have a lot of

clips.text: Sometimes people have a lot of really scary emotions inside and those emotions can express themselves in different ways. A lot of people choose to draw or to smush clay or something like that and shape it into different things when they have those emotions. But some people never learned how to do that and so they start to smush things or push things or press things that might have emotions themselves and that's not really good because then what happens is other people will get upset and then they'll have a lot of strong emotions and they won't know how to express those emotions either and so they'll start to express them in different places. Places that art shouldn't usually go and that can be a really big problem but it's also okay that that happens.

clips.text: Hey guys, it's WhistlerGraph. I want to talk about some college-level media theory. So if Google was the interface of the Internet of the 90s and the 10s, you know, the text box, it assumed that you knew what to search for. But most people actually don't really know what they want. And so, TikTokers are basically human information clerks. We mostly tell you what to Google. And it's the new front door of Gen Z's Internet, soon to be everyone else's.
